figures.caption: Figure 3: The generation consistency curve w.r.t. the action sequence length. Eight world models or video generators execute identical 9-round action sequences from the same initial observations, with consistency scores measured at each round. Most models achieve lower than 75% consistency after round 5 or 6, indicating fundamental limitations for long-horizon planning applications.
Figure 1: The different focus of existing world model and our evaluation benchmarks.

Figure 5: Qualitative comparison on Simulation Fidelity. Given a shared initial driving scene (S0), PAN, Cosmos 1, and Cosmos 2 simulate a three-step sequence of environmental interventions from a dry forest road to light rain to intensifying rainfall.

Figure 4: Qualitative comparison on Structured Simulation and Planning. Starting from the same tabletop observation (S0) with the goal of arranging all blocks into a line, PAN, Cosmos 1, and Cosmos 2 each simulate multi-step action plans through an iterative VLM–world model planning loop.

Table 1: Model performance on Action Simulation Fidelity, Long-horizon Forecast and Simulative Reasoning & Planning tasks. Results in Action Simulation Fidelity are measured based on the criteria proposed in Li et al. (2025) and then normalized. Task success rates in Open-Ended Sim. and Struct. Sim. are measured in the trajectory level and represent improvements over the pure VLM agent without integrating WM. The best-performing model in each dimension is marked in bold, and the second best is underlined.
Figure 2: Taxonomy with detailed examples of our evaluation benchmark.
